task_completion.description: Log into the email client using the provided credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the graphical email client interface successfully logged in and displaying the inbox.

task_completion.description: Identify and select the duplicate emails in the inbox.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the inbox with multiple identical emails visible, and the agent selecting or highlighting the duplicate copies.

task_completion.description: Remove the duplicate emails, preserving exactly one copy of every unique message.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the duplicates being deleted. Evidence includes the inbox message count reducing to 25, or the trash/deleted items folder containing the 8 removed duplicate emails.

task_completion.description: Draft an incident report email with the correct recipient and subject.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An email composition window is open with the 'To' field set exactly to 'it-infrastructure@company.com' and the 'Subject' field set to 'Duplicate Sync Incident'.

task_completion.description: Include the correct count of removed messages in the drafted email body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The body of the drafted email states that duplicates were found and removed, and explicitly includes the number '8' as the total count of removed messages.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical email client to manage emails.
integrity.visual_evidence: The deletion of emails and drafting of the report occur within a GUI email client (e.g., Thunderbird, Evolution), not via terminal commands manipulating the Maildir directly.

integrity.description: The incident report is drafted in a real email client.
integrity.visual_evidence: The drafted email is shown in the email client's actual composition window, not in a basic text editor like gedit or nano.

integrity.description: The agent did not use scripts to bypass manual GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing Python or bash scripts being executed to automatically find/delete duplicates or send the email.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The inbox initially contains 33 emails: 25 unique emails and 8 duplicates. The agent must delete exactly 8 duplicate emails, leaving 25 unique emails in the inbox. The drafted incident report must be addressed to 'it-infrastructure@company.com', have the subject 'Duplicate Sync Incident', and explicitly state that exactly 8 messages were removed.
